2603.19092.title: SAVeS: Steering Safety Judgments in Vision-Language Models via Semantic Cues
2603.19092.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world and embodied settings where safety decisions depend on visual context. However, it remains unclear which visual evidence drives these judgments. We study whether multimodal safety behavior in VLMs can be steered by simple semantic cues. We introduce a semantic steering framework that applies controlled textual, visual, and cognitive interventions without changing the underlying scene content. To evaluate these effects, we propose SAVeS, a benchmark for situational safety under semantic cues, together with an evaluation protocol that separates behavioral refusal, grounded safety reasoning, and false refusals. Experiments across multiple VLMs and an additional state-of-the-art benchmark show that safety decisions are highly sensitive to semantic cues, indicating reliance on learned visual-linguistic associations rather than grounded visual understanding. We further demonstrate that automated steering pipelines can exploit these mechanisms, highlighting a potential vulnerability in multimodal safety systems.
2603.19092.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型 (VLMs) 正在越來越多地應用於現實世界和具體環境中，這些環境中的安全決策依賴於視覺上下文。<lb/>然而，尚不清楚哪些視覺證據驅動這些判斷。<lb/>我們研究了 VLMs 中的多模態安全行為是否可以通過簡單的語義提示來引導。<lb/>我們引入了一個語義引導框架，該框架在不改變基礎場景內容的情況下應用受控的文本、視覺和認知干預。<lb/>為了評估這些效果，我們提出了 SAVeS，一個在語義提示下的情境安全基準，以及一個評估協議，該協議將行為拒絕、基於事實的安全推理和錯誤拒絕分開。<lb/>在多個 VLMs 和一個額外的最先進基準上的實驗顯示，安全決策對語義提示非常敏感，這表明其依賴於學習到的視覺-語言聯結，而不是基於事實的視覺理解。<lb/>我們進一步證明，自動化引導管道可以利用這些機制，突顯出多模態安全系統中的潛在脆弱性。
2603.19092.author: Carlos Hinojosa et.al.
2603.19092.authors: Carlos Hinojosa, Clemens Grange, Bernard Ghanem
